mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing market signal, as indicated by a 2-month increase in HOUST, triggers an expansionary demand cycle that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate signal, as indicated by a 1-month decrease in UNRATE, triggers an expansionary labor cycle that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the industrial production signal, as indicated by a 1-month increase in INDPRO, triggers an expansionary supply cycle that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the monetary policy rate signal, as indicated by a 1-month decrease in FEDFUNDS, triggers an easing monetary cycle that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the retail sales signal, as indicated by a 1-month increase in RSAFS, triggers an expansionary demand cycle that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the labor force participation rate signal, as indicated by a 1-month decrease in PAYEMS, triggers an expansionary labor cycle that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-month rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the industrial production signal, as indicated by a 1-month increase in INDPRO, triggers an expansionary supply cycle that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the monetary policy rate signal, as indicated by a 1-month decrease in FEDFUNDS, triggers an easing monetary cycle that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
